properties.rationale: Highest vessel density in New Zealand with major marinas (Westhaven, Half Moon Bay, Gulf Harbour, Bayswater). Population of 1.7M+ drives massive recreational boating pressure. Direct connectivity to all confirmed Caulerpa CAN sites via regular vessel traffic. Marine reserves (Long Bay-Okura, Motu Manawa-Pollen Island) and HPAs (Rangitoto/Motutapu, Tiritiri Matangi) add ecological consequence.
key_factors: Direct vessel connectivity to confirmed CAN sites
Long Bay-Okura and Motu Manawa marine reserves
Sheltered coastline with suitable Caulerpa habitat
data_sources: restricted_zones.geojson (Long Bay-Okura MR, Motu Manawa MR, Rangitoto/Motutapu HPA, Tiritiri Matangi HPA)
iNaturalist observations (Hauraki Gulf Central/Waiheke cluster)
can_areas.geojson (Waiheke CAN proximity)

properties.override: Protected areas and confirmed infestations — elevated to High. Multiple HPAs, marine reserves, and confirmed exotic Caulerpa CAN zones across Waiheke, Aotea/Great Barrier, and Hauturu/Little Barrier.
properties.rationale: Epicentre of confirmed exotic Caulerpa in New Zealand. Three of five confirmed CAN sites are in this zone (Waiheke, Aotea/Great Barrier, Hauturu/Little Barrier). 30 exotic iNaturalist observations recorded. Multiple HPAs (The Noises, Motukawao, Pakatoa/Tarahiki) and Te Matuku Marine Reserve add ecological consequence. High vessel traffic from Auckland ferries and recreational boats.
key_factors: Three confirmed CAN sites (Waiheke, Aotea, Hauturu)
Heavy ferry and recreational vessel traffic
Sheltered bays with ideal Caulerpa habitat
data_sources: can_areas.geojson (Waiheke CAN, Aotea/Great Barrier CAN zones, Hauturu CAN, Ahuahu CAN)
restricted_zones.geojson (Te Matuku MR, The Noises HPA, Motukawao HPA, Pakatoa/Tarahiki HPA, Hauturu HPA)
iNaturalist observations (80 obs Hauraki Gulf North, 56 obs Central/Waiheke)
known_infestations: Waiheke Island — Onetangi Bay (C. brachypus/parvifolia), confirmed CAN
Aotea/Great Barrier Island (C. brachypus, C. parvifolia), confirmed CAN with red and yellow zones
Hauturu/Little Barrier Island, confirmed CAN red zone

properties.override: Active rāhui (Ōmaha Bay Temporary Closure) and marine reserves elevate to High. Cape Rodney-Okakari Point and Tawharanui marine reserves, Kawau Bay HPA present.
properties.rationale: Active rāhui from Cape Rodney to Tāwharanui signals iwi concern and ecological sensitivity. Two significant marine reserves (Cape Rodney-Okakari Point, Tawharanui) and Kawau Bay HPA. Popular weekend anchoring and boating destination with direct vessel traffic from Auckland. Proximity to Hauraki Gulf island CAN sites creates spread corridor.
key_factors: Active Ōmaha Bay rāhui (Cape Rodney to Tāwharanui)
Vessel corridor from Auckland to Hauraki Gulf
data_sources: rahui.geojson (Ōmaha Bay Temporary Closure, lat -36.362 to -36.279)
restricted_zones.geojson (Cape Rodney-Okakari Point MR, Tawharanui MR, Kawau Bay HPA)
can_areas.geojson (proximity to Gulf island CAN zones)

properties.rationale: Eastern Coromandel coast has good Caulerpa habitat in sheltered bays and moderate vessel traffic from Auckland recreational boats. Cathedral Cove Marine Reserve, Cape Colville HPA, Slipper Island HPA, and Aldermen Islands HPA add ecological value. Ahuahu/Great Mercury Island confirmed CAN is immediately offshore. Seasonal tourism brings elevated vessel traffic in summer.
key_factors: Cape Colville, Slipper Island, Aldermen Islands HPAs
data_sources: restricted_zones.geojson (Cathedral Cove MR, Cape Colville HPA, Slipper Island HPA, Aldermen Islands HPA)
can_areas.geojson (Ahuahu/Great Mercury Island CAN)
iNaturalist observations (Hauraki Gulf South/Coromandel North)

properties.rationale: Te Rāwhiti confirmed CAN red zone is within this area — one of the earliest confirmed exotic Caulerpa sites in New Zealand. Intense vessel traffic from charter boats, cruise ships, and recreational boats. 141 iNaturalist observations including 4 exotic records. Warm sheltered waters provide ideal Caulerpa habitat. Strong iwi values throughout the Bay of Islands. Major tourism hub with international vessels.
key_factors: Te Rāwhiti confirmed exotic Caulerpa CAN (red zone)
Warm sheltered waters — ideal Caulerpa habitat
Strong iwi values and cultural significance
data_sources: can_areas.geojson (Te Rāwhiti Exotic Caulerpa High Risk Zone, lat -35.262 to -35.227)
iNaturalist observations (Far North cluster, 141 obs, 4 exotic)
known_infestations: Te Rāwhiti, Bay of Islands — confirmed exotic Caulerpa CAN red zone

properties.rationale: Deep sheltered harbour used as vessel refuge and popular anchorage. Very close proximity to Bay of Islands confirmed CAN site creates high spread risk via vessel movement. Northern location provides warm water temperatures and high environmental suitability for exotic Caulerpa. Game fishing hub attracts vessels from across Northland.
key_factors: Sheltered harbour — vessel refuge and anchorage
Close proximity to Bay of Islands CAN site
Game fishing hub with vessel traffic
Soft sediment habitat suitable for Caulerpa
Connected vessel traffic patterns with Far North
data_sources: can_areas.geojson (Te Rāwhiti CAN proximity)
iNaturalist observations (Far North/Whangaroa cluster)

properties.rationale: Marsden Point is a major commercial port and oil refinery wharf, bringing domestic and international vessel traffic. Regional spread hub between Auckland and Far North confirmed CAN sites. Aquaculture operations (mussel/oyster) in the area increase consequence of infestation. Town Basin marina is a key recreational hub for Northland.
key_factors: Regional spread hub (Auckland — Far North corridor)
Aquaculture operations in Bream Bay area
Town Basin marina — Northland recreational hub
data_sources: iNaturalist observations (Whangarei/Bream Bay, 8 obs, 0 exotic)
can_areas.geojson (positioned between Auckland and Far North CAN sites)

properties.rationale: New Zealand's largest export port by volume with major international vessel traffic and ballast water risk. Tauranga Bridge Marina and adjacent marinas provide significant recreational vessel density. Fast-growing population centre (190K+). Wider Bay of Plenty has mussel farming and aquaculture operations. Sheltered harbour with suitable Caulerpa habitat.
key_factors: NZ's largest export port (international vessel/ballast risk)
Tauranga Bridge Marina and recreational fleet
Sheltered harbour with soft sediment habitat
1 exotic iNat observation south of Tauranga
data_sources: iNaturalist observations (south of Tauranga, 418 obs, 1 exotic)

properties.rationale: New Zealand's largest harbour by area with extensive soft sediment and sheltered waters providing excellent Caulerpa habitat. Significant iwi and customary fishing areas throughout. Established oyster farming industry at risk. Lower vessel traffic than east coast harbours reduces introduction risk, but any introduction would be difficult to manage given the harbour's size.
key_factors: Largest harbour in NZ — extensive Caulerpa habitat
Lower vessel traffic than east coast
Difficult to manage if Caulerpa established
data_sources: General regional assessment (no specific Caulerpa observations)

properties.override: Iwi significance and proximity to confirmed Gulf island infestations — minimum Medium maintained. Additional risk factors from vessel traffic and habitat suitability are present but do not reach High threshold.
properties.rationale: Sheltered harbour close to confirmed Hauraki Gulf island infestations. Popular mooring and anchoring area with regular vessel traffic from Auckland. Historical oyster farming area. Vessels transiting between Auckland and the outer Hauraki Gulf pass through or near this area. Iwi significance of the harbour and surrounding coast.
key_factors: Close proximity to Gulf island CAN sites
Vessel transit corridor to outer Gulf
data_sources: can_areas.geojson (proximity to Aotea, Hauturu, Waiheke CAN zones)
restricted_zones.geojson (nearby Kawau Bay HPA)

properties.rationale: Westhaven Marina is the largest marina in the southern hemisphere and the primary vessel hub connecting all regions of the upper North Island. All recreational and many commercial vessels transit through the Waitematā. Motu Manawa-Pollen Island Marine Reserve is within the harbour. Highest population exposure of any zone. Any Caulerpa introduction here would have maximum spread potential via vessel traffic to every other zone.
key_factors: Westhaven Marina — largest in southern hemisphere
Primary vessel hub connecting all regions
Highest spread potential via vessel traffic
data_sources: restricted_zones.geojson (Motu Manawa-Pollen Island MR, lat -36.873 to -36.852)
can_areas.geojson (vessel connectivity to all CAN sites)

properties.rationale: Connecting waterway between confirmed Waiheke CAN site and the wider Hauraki Gulf. Significant mussel and oyster aquaculture in the Firth of Thames. Thames/Kopu commercial wharf provides vessel introduction pathway. Miranda/Firth wetlands have high iwi significance. Sheltered shallow waters with extensive soft sediment provide good Caulerpa habitat across a large area.
data_sources: can_areas.geojson (Waiheke CAN connectivity)
iNaturalist observations (Hauraki Gulf South area)

properties.rationale: High-energy exposed west coast with heavy swells and strong currents provide poor Caulerpa habitat. Very low vessel density with no significant marinas or anchorages along this stretch. Included as a low-priority comparative reference zone to calibrate the scoring model against areas of genuinely low risk. Rocky exposed coastline with limited soft sediment.
data_sources: General regional assessment (comparative reference)

properties.rationale: Large enclosed harbour on Auckland's west coast. International airport proximity means ballast water and biofouling risk from nearby Onehunga port. Iwi significant to Tainui with extensive customary fishing areas. Lower recreational vessel density than east coast harbours but still substantial given Auckland population. Harbour has areas of suitable Caulerpa habitat in sheltered upper reaches.
key_factors: Lower recreational vessel density than east coast
Suitable habitat in sheltered upper reaches
data_sources: General regional assessment

properties.override: Mokohinau Islands HPA — protected area override elevates minimum priority to Medium.
properties.rationale: Remote outer Gulf waters centred on Mokohinau Islands HPA. Connected to confirmed CAN sites via recreational and fishing vessel traffic — potential stepping stone for Caulerpa spread between Hauturu/Little Barrier and the wider Gulf. More exposed than inner Gulf but Mokohinau group provides some sheltered habitat. Lower vessel traffic than inner Gulf but regular fishing and diving visits.
key_factors: Connected via vessel traffic to CAN sites
Regular fishing and diving vessel visits
data_sources: restricted_zones.geojson (Mokohinau Islands HPA)
can_areas.geojson (Hauturu CAN proximity)
